headline: French architects of Iran nuclear deal watch Trump unravel their work
description: French diplomats who helped negotiate the 2015 Vienna agreement, aimed to prevent Tehran from acquiring nuclear weapons, now watch bitterly as decisions by the Trump administration unravel their work.

headline: Israel PM says targeted a top Hezbollah commander in Beirut strike
Gaza police become targets for Israeli army
Court overturns dismissal of former Rwandan first lady's 'complicity in genocide' charges
description: The Paris appeals court ruled that the investigation into Agathe Habyarimana must resume. She is accused of acts that constitute 'complicity in genocide and crimes against humanity' during the Rwandan genocide.

headline: Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
description: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

headline: PSG-Bayern Munich: What makes a match legendary?
description: The celebrations surrounding the spectacular Champions League match between Paris Saint-Germain and Bayern Munich reveal the growing dominance of entertainment over competition, writes journalist Jérôme Latta in his column.

headline: Ted Turner, founder of CNN, is dead
description: The four-time 'Yachtsman of the Year' relished a challenge. When he launched Cable News Network in Atlanta in 1980, few believed the all-news channel would succeed. Also a philanthropist and founder of the United Nations Foundation, Turner died at the age of 87.

headline: German Chancellor Merz hits a new low one year after taking office
On Algeria, Macron has a goal for his last year in office: Reconciliation
'The Gulf cold war is set to give the other petro-monarchies cold sweats'
Trump temporarily backs down on reopening the Strait of Hormuz by force
Vincent Duclert, Rwandan genocide specialist: 'In the Agathe Habyarimana case, the judiciary must review all available documentation'
French PM resolves to step up economic aid as Middle East conflict drags on
EU auditors sound alarm over billions in untraceable Covid recovery funds
EU urged to 'act now' to stop West Bank settlement project
How Bruno Retailleau is staging his break with Macron over Algeria
Plaintiffs' lawyers denounce 'inversion of victimhood' at Sarkozy's appeal trial
Romania plunges back into political chaos after Social Democrats and far right pass no-confidence motion
Sweden's conservatives are now normalizing the far-right
Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
description: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

headline: Hundreds of flight cancellations leave passengers frustrated and stranded
description: The war in the Middle East has caused the price of jet fuel to double in just a few weeks. Many airlines, especially low-cost carriers, have canceled flights with low occupancy or low profitability. Passenger rights are protected, but within certain limits.

headline: Europeans rethink collective defense amid Trump's threats against NATO
description: Would the US intervene in the event of an attack on Europe? European leaders, who met Monday in Yerevan alongside Canada, are growing increasingly doubtful after Trump announced the withdrawal of 5,000 American troops from Germany.

headline: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
'With the withdrawal of US troops from Germany, Berlin is facing a historic dilemma'
War exposes democratic erosion in the US
